Title: Myb10-D confers PHS-3D resistance to pre-harvest sprouting by regulating NCED in ABA biosynthesis pathway of wheat

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Promotes seed dormancy and delays germination, contributing to pre-harvest sprouting resistance
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Expressed during 5–30 DPA seed development; upregulated in Myb10-D overexpression lines
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Increased NCED transcription is associated with increased ABA accumulation and delayed germination/PHS resistance
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qPCR; RNA-seq; EMSA; dual-luciferase transient assay in Nicotiana benthamiana and Arabidopsis protoplasts; yeast one-hybrid; ABA metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Cleaves 9-cis-violaxanthin and 9'-cis-neoxanthin to produce xanthoxin, the precursor of ABA
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point; committed-step enzyme for ABA biosynthesis as supported by the paper
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper demonstrates that Myb10-D directly binds the SMRE motif at -192 to -199 bp in the NCED promoter and activates transcription
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: A useful downstream target/mechanistic node for improving seed dormancy and PHS resistance; can inform marker-assisted selection or engineering of ABA-mediated dormancy
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. NCED was one of the ABA-pathway genes upregulated in Myb10-D overexpression wheat. 2. Myb10-D directly bound the NCED promoter SMRE motif and activated its transcription in EMSA, dual-luciferase, and yeast one-hybrid assays. 3. Elevated NCED expression correlated with higher ABA accumulation and delayed germination/PHS resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; metabolomics/ABA profiling; NCBI SRA: PRJNA605937
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher ABA and stronger dormancy may slow germination, which could be undesirable for rapid, uniform establishment if excessive

Regulation_Genes.Source_Species: Aegilops tauschii-derived D-genome wheat allele / wheat D-genome locus
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: ABA promotes seed dormancy and delays germination; flavonoids contribute to grain color and reduced seed coat permeability
Regulation_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Developing seeds; mesocarp of the inner pericarp/seed tissues
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in seed, especially early seed development and aleurone/pericarp-associated tissues
Regulation_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highly expressed in seeds at 5–15 DPA; concentrated mainly in the mesocarp of the inner pericarp in transgenic wheat; absent in deletion lines
Regulation_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Confers PHS resistance by delaying germination, increasing ABA and flavonoid accumulation, decreasing seed coat permeability, and causing red grain color
Regulation_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Fine mapping in HIF population; association/haplotype analysis in worldwide germplasm; transgenic overexpression in wheat; RNA-seq; metabolomics; RNA in situ hybridization; subcellular localization; EMSA; dual-luciferase assay; yeast one-hybrid; qPCR
Regulation_Genes.Primary_Regulatory_Targets: NCED; flavonoid pathway genes CHS; CHI; F3H; GT; DFR; ANS; F3'H/F30'H; UFGT
Regulation_Genes.Regulatory_Effect_on_Target_Genes: Activates NCED directly; upregulates multiple flavonoid biosynthetic genes
Regulation_Genes.Metabolic_Process_Controlled: ABA biosynthesis; flavonoid biosynthesis; seed dormancy/germination control; grain pigmentation
Regulation_Genes.Decisive_Influence_on_Target_Product: Major causal regulator underlying PHS-3D; pleiotropic regulator linking grain color and seed dormancy/PHS resistance
Regulation_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Seed developmental stage (5–15 DPA; 5–30 DPA); imbibition/germination assays
Regulation_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Located in a 2.4 Mb presence–absence variation region on chromosome 3DL. Presence of Myb10-D is associated with ~40% lower germination percentage in global wheat lines. It is also the known R-1 grain color gene with dual function in flavonoid and ABA pathways.
Regulation_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High-value gene for improving PHS resistance; useful for marker-assisted selection and engineering of dormancy, though linked/pleiotropic red grain color effect should be considered when breeding white-grained resistant wheat
Regulation_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Myb10-D was identified as the likely causal gene in the PHS-3D PAV region by integrated mapping and omics. 2. Overexpression caused red grain, lower seed coat permeability, increased ABA/flavonoids, and 38.5–55.1% lower germination than WT. 3. Myb10-D directly activated NCED by binding an SMRE motif in its promoter, explaining enhanced ABA biosynthesis and delayed germination.
Regulation_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq; metabolomics/flavonoid profiling; ABA profiling; genomic comparison/pangenome analysis; NCBI SRA: PRJNA605937
Regulation_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Pleiotropic induction of red grain color and stronger dormancy may complicate breeding for white-grained cultivars with rapid post-sowing germination
Regulation_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Maize/polyubiquitin promoter Ubi1 for wheat overexpression; 35S promoter in transient assays
